howItWorks.title: How Yearly Leave Reset Works
howItWorks.point1: Calculates leave balances for the selected year based on current leave policies.
howItWorks.point2: Applies carryover rules automatically within allowed limits.
howItWorks.point3: Approved payroll periods are protected and will not be changed.
howItWorks.multiBranch: Branch policies are ignored for employees assigned to multiple branches.

leaveReset.partialSuccess: Yearly reset completed, but {{count}} users were skipped due to approved payroll.
leaveReset.runTooltip: This will recalculate leave balances for all active employees for the selected year. Approved payroll periods will not be affected.
leaveReset.title: Yearly Leave Reset
leaveReset.warning: Running yearly reset will recalculate leave balances based on current leave policies and carryover rules. This action is safe and will not modify approved payroll periods.
leaveReset.noPreview: No leave balances exist for this year yet. You can run the yearly reset to generate them.

leavePolicies.carryoverHint: Maximum number of unused annual leave days that can be carried to the next year.
leavePolicies.carryoverAppliedOnReset: Carryover is applied only during the yearly leave reset.

rules.title: Leave Policy Rules
rules.leaveYearResetNote1: Annual and sick leave balances are automatically reset at the beginning of each year based on the employee's timezone, leave policy, and carryover rules.
rules.priority: Effective policy priority: User → Role → Branch → Global.
rules.branches: An employee who works in more than one branch is not affected by any of the branch policies but is subject to the global policy, the job role policy, or a policy specific to him, according to priority.
rules.noAutoBalance: Policies do not automatically change balances.
rules.futureOnly: Policy changes affect future calculations only.
rules.yearBased: Each employee has a separate balance per year (UserLeaveYear).
rules.carryover: Carryover is applied only during yearly recalculation.
rules.sick: Sick leave resets yearly with no carryover.
rules.payrollLock: Balances cannot be adjusted if payroll is approved for the same year.
rules.audit: All manual adjustments are recorded in the audit log.

notes.branchPriority: An employee who works in more than one branch is not affected by any of the branch policies but is subject to the global policy, the job role policy, or a policy specific to him, according to priority.

attendancePolicy.noEffectivePolicyToday: No effective attendance policy is applied today. This usually means there is no valid check-in and check-out yet.
attendancePolicy.ruleValidBranch: Branch policy is applied only after a valid check-in and check-out that is not invalidated
attendancePolicy.ruleFirstBranch: An employee who works in more than one branch is not affected by any of the branch policies but is subject to the global policy, the job role policy, or a policy specific to him, according to priority.
attendancePolicy.ruleDisplayOnly: Displayed branch policies do not affect payroll unless valid attendance exists
attendancePolicy.effectivePolicyExplanation: This policy represents the final calculated attendance policy applied to the employee for today based on attendance records and priority rules.
attendancePolicy.sourceFirstValidCheckin: First valid attendance of the day

errors.invalidDayRate: Day deduction rate must be between 0 and 1
